rules.rationale: In-app browsers stall/kill local inference regardless of hardware (LINE IAB, Pixel 8a 2026-06-10). NOTE: IAB does NOT hide WebGPU — the archived LINE-IAB probe export (12:43, runs empty) shows webgpu:true with a fully visible adapter, so R2 cannot be relied on to catch IABs; UA detection here is load-bearing. UA-list misses are accepted v0 residual risk, self-healed by the strike rule (first stall → tombstone → strike → server).
No WebGPU → no local path (WebLLM is WebGPU-only).

0 successful iOS rows in Gate 0. iPhone 11 Pro Max (4GB, Safari 26.5): Qwen1.5B died at 728MB fetched (TypeError + silent tab kill x4); wllama 0.5B init-killed x2; WebLLM Llama ctx2048 generate-killed. Device RAM is not detectable on iOS → conservative all-iOS server in v0. Includes iPadOS masquerading as desktop (MacIntel + maxTouchPoints>1).
Desktop WebGPU: 121-196 tps decode, prefill ~2.6k tps (Gate 0). est_prompt_tokens <= 3000 guard keeps prompts inside the default 4096-token local KV window (B-4); overflow routes to server.

Pixel 8a: decode ~10 tps OK, prefill ~15 tps → long context unusable (77s TTFT @1.2k tok). The 200-token threshold is a 2-point interpolation between measured short (~52 tok) and long (~1213 tok) prompts — the 200-token region itself is UNMEASURED. stream:true is required (Q6): a non-streaming 256-token generation at ~10 tps blocks ~26s with zero feedback, which is perceived failure; non-streaming Android requests go to server.
